task_completion.description: Select 'Brown v. Board of Education' in the Jurism library
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jurism application, the case 'Brown v. Board of Education' is highlighted or selected in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: Set the Short Title for 'Brown v. Board of Education' to 'Brown'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right-hand pane for 'Brown v. Board of Education', the 'Short Title' field contains the text 'Brown'.

task_completion.description: Select 'Miranda v. Arizona' in the Jurism library
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jurism application, the case 'Miranda v. Arizona' is highlighted or selected in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: Set the Short Title for 'Miranda v. Arizona' to 'Miranda'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right-hand pane for 'Miranda v. Arizona', the 'Short Title' field contains the text 'Miranda'.

task_completion.description: Select 'Obergefell v. Hodges' in the Jurism library
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jurism application, the case 'Obergefell v. Hodges' is highlighted or selected in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: Set the Short Title for 'Obergefell v. Hodges' to 'Obergefell'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right-hand pane for 'Obergefell v. Hodges', the 'Short Title' field contains the text 'Obergefell'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jurism GUI to make the changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Jurism graphical user interface being used to edit the fields, rather than terminal commands, database scripts, or a text editor.
Other fields in the right-hand pane (such as Case Name, Reporter, Date Decided) for the three cases remain unchanged and match the original case details.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Jurism application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being interacted with is clearly Jurism, displaying its standard three-pane layout with the correct library items.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
The task requires setting the 'Short Title' field for three specific Supreme Court cases in Jurism. The expected short titles are:
1. Brown v. Board of Education (1954) -> 'Brown'
2. Miranda v. Arizona (1966) -> 'Miranda'
3. Obergefell v. Hodges (2015) -> 'Obergefell'
The Short Title field is located in the right-hand pane of the Jurism application when a case is selected.
